title: Infinite Loops & Break
description: Loops that run forever, and how to stop them.

slides.title: Infinity and Beyond 🚀
slides.content:
## The Loop That Never Ends

Sometimes, you want a loop to run forever.
For example:
*   A video game waiting for a player's controller input.
*   A web server perpetually listening for incoming connections.

In Go, an empty `for` statement runs forever.

slides.title: Forever 'For' ♾️
slides.content: To write an infinite loop, you literally just type `for {}` with no condition at all!

quizData.question: What keyword is used to instantly stop a loop and escape from it?
quizData.explanation: The `break` keyword immediately "breaks out" of the loop, skipping any remaining code inside it.

quizData.question: To write an infinite loop in Go, you simply use the keyword `_____` with no condition.
quizData.explanation: An empty `for {}` evaluates as a `for true {}` and runs infinitely.
